figures.caption: Figure 1: Numerical results of Algorithm 1 for LQ-1, -2, and -3 in section 4.1 with d = 1. In the first column, scattered points show simulated paths of XµI ,uα

Figure 5: Results of Algorithm 1 for the MFG in section 4.3. (Upper left) Loss versus iteration. (Upper right) Empirical value Ĵ of the cost functional in (4) versus iteration. (Lower left) Overlay of snapshots of agent positions at t = i/5T , i = 0, 1, · · · , 5. (Lower right) Agent trajectories with snapshots at t = i/5T , i = 0, 1, · · · , 5; the red x marks the target position z∗(T ), and the yellow ”o” denotes the barrier point.

Table 1: Numerical results of Algorithm 1 for LQ-1, -2, and -3 in section 4.1. In the RE1, RE∞, and RC columns, values outside and inside parentheses are the mean and standard deviation over 5 independent runs, respectively. In the MEM column, values report the peak single-GPU memory usage per iteration, in megabytes (MB), multiplied by 8 to obtain the total usage across 8 GPUs. Convergence histories are shown in Figures 1 and 2.

Figure 4: Results of Algorithm 1 for the MFG in section 4.2. (Upper left) Loss versus iteration. (Upper right) Empirical value Ĵ of the cost functional in (4) versus iteration. (Lower left) Overlay of snapshots of agent positions at t = i/5T , i = 0, 1, · · · , 5. (Lower right) Agent trajectories over time, with snapshots at t = i/5T , i = 0, 1, · · · , 5; the red x marks the target position z∗(T ).

Figure 3: Numerical results of Algorithm 1 for LQ-1 in section 4.1 with d = 10,000. The left subplot compares the exact and predicted values of s 7→ v(0, s1d). The middle and right subplots show RE and RC versus iteration. The runtime is 5258 seconds, and the peak per-iteration single-GPU memory usage is 55,645 MB, multiplied by 8 to obtain the total usage across 8 GPUs.

Figure 2: Numerical results of Algorithm 1 for LQ-1, -2, and -3 in section 4.1 with d = 1, 000. The first column shows the exact and predicted values of s 7→ v(0, s1d). In the second to fourth columns, the shaded regions show the mean plus 2× SD over five independent runs. Loss in the last-column heading denotes the absolute value of the right-hand side of (33). Table 1 summarizes the final quantitative results.
